metadata.title: Meezan Roshan Resident Account | Meezan Bank
metadata.description: Meezan Roshan Resident Account has been specifically designed to facilitate Resident Pakistanis who have declared assets held abroad, as per wealth statement declared in the latest tax returns with Federal Board of Revenue (FBR).

content: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Roshan Resident Account For Resident Pakistanis With Declared Assets Abroad! If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Specifically designed to facilitate Resident Pakistanis Roshan Resident Account has been specifically facilitate Resident Pakistanis who have declared assets held abroad, as per wealth statement declared in the latest tax returns with Federal Board of Revenue (FBR). It enables Resident Pakistanis to invest in the Shariah-compliant Foreign Currency (FCY) denominated scheme of Islamic Naya Pakistan Certificates (INPCs) and Meezan Bank's FCY Term Deposit products. Cash withdrawal in foreign currency and equivalent local currency can also be made through this account. Terms & Conditions Account can ONLY be opened by visiting Meezan Bank branches that are authorized to open Foreign Currency Accounts (list of branches attached) Roshan Resident Account can be opened in USD, GBP & EUR currencies only. Both Savings & Current accounts can be opened. Normal account opening procedure will be followed. No local deposits are allowed except profit/proceeds from investments as permitted by State Bank of Pakistan. In addition to standard documentation required for Know Your Customer/Customer Due Deligence (KYC/CDD), customer will provide copy of latest tax returns including wealth statement filed of Revenue (FBR). The declared/disclosed assets held abroad must be reflected in latest wealth statement. Previous year wealth statements will not be accepted. Account can be fed upto the maximum amount of assets declared with FBR latest wealth statement. Repatriation i.e. remittances and payments outside Pakistan can be made to the extent of balances available in the account, without any prior approval. Key Features Online Branch Banking Free Online Banking service at all Meezan Bank branches No Initial Deposit No initial deposit for account opening Minimum Balance No minimum balance requirement Chequebook Issuance of chequebook in accordance with the Schedule of Charges (SOCs) Free Pay Orders No charges on issuance of Pay Orders Profit Payment & Calculation Profit will be paid on monthly basis & calculated on daily basis Withdrawals No restriction on withdrawals amount Funds Transfer Instant Funds Transfer within Meezan Bank FAQs How does Roshan Resident Account work? Download Fatwa Roshan Resident Account is offered in both current & savings accounts under the Islamic concept of Qard & Mudarabah. Profit Sharing Ratio USD Savings Account Bank will share 75% of Gross Income as Mudarib Depositor will share 25% Gross Income as Rab-ul-Maal GBP Bank will share 90% Depositor will share 10% Income as Rab-ul-Maal EUR Income as Rab-ul-Maal Weightages for the Month of April 2026 Products Tier groups Weightage Assigned Roshan Resident Account USD Monthly 0.58 Roshan Resident Account GBP Monthly 0.27 Roshan Resident Account EUR Monthly 0.27 Consolidated Weightages Profit Rates Profit Rates the Month of February 2026 Products Tenure Profit Account USD Monthly 2.00% Account GBP Monthly 1.25% Account EUR Monthly 0.97% Profit Rates Historical Profit Rates Mudarabah Basis On agreeing to become a US Dollar Account holder, the customer enters into a relationship based on Mudarabah with Meezan Bank. Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the funds deposited by the customers. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; funds from the pool are utilized to provide financing to customers under Islamic modes that include, but are not restricted to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharakah. Qard Basis Current Account (US Dollar) is based on Qard contract. The Bank can use these funds for investment and other purposes. This account allows you the peace of mind of having your money safely deposited with a bank with the additional assurance that the bank is not investing your money in activities that contravene Shariah principles.
